Silent save: use-podcast-settings.ts:271, use-voiceover-settings.ts:107-108, use-infographic-settings.ts:175. title: Deploy script blocks invalid CORS wildcard and surfaces failed service logs
trigger: Deploy failed after image builds when server exited during startup
finding: Root cause was CORS_ORIGINS=* in production compose; server enforces credentialed CORS and exited. Updated deploy to normalize '*' to web origin and print exited-service logs on startup failures.
evidence: deploy functions normalize_cors_origins + print_failed_service_logs; docker logs showed: CORS_ORIGINS=* is only allowed for credentialed CORS in local development.
followUp: Consider writing normalized runtime values back into .env.deploy and optionally adding a --remove-orphans path for compose runs.
reflection: Inspecting container logs immediately after compose failure isolated the config root cause and enabled a narrow script fix.
feedback: Deploy should always emit targeted startup diagnostics so failures are actionable without manual docker log commands.

title: Guardrail against paranoid fallback branches
trigger: Request to update AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md and canonical docs guidance
finding: Added explicit rule to avoid speculative fallback code paths; require typed explicit failures unless graceful fallback is product-defined.
followUp: Apply this rule in future PR reviews for backend/frontend error handling changes.
reflection: Single-source docs update kept instruction files aligned with canonical pattern docs.
feedback: Keep short examples when adding new policy to avoid interpretation drift.

title: Deploy reliability follow-up: web ownership and healthcheck fixes
trigger: Post-fix deploy still showed web startup/runtime issues
finding: Resolved two additional deployment blockers: web entrypoint needed writable dist ownership, and compose web healthcheck had to probe 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost.
evidence: apps/web/Dockerfile now copies dist/serve.js/entrypoint.sh with --chown=app:app and uses 127.0.0.1 healthcheck; compose.yaml web healthcheck also updated to 127.0.0.1. Deploy now reaches Ready 7/7.
followUp: If desired, add --remove-orphans option toggle in deploy to clear db-test orphan warnings.
reflection: Iterative verification with real compose runs caught environment-specific issues that unit-level checks miss.
feedback: When deploy scripts change, include one end-to-end container health verification pass before closing.

title: Product vision: community persona library with browse, publish, and clone
trigger: Human-directed product-vision-researcher run focused on marketplace/library concept for personas and style presets
finding: Persona creation is the highest-friction step in podcast workflows. Every comparable platform (GPT Store, Character.ai, ElevenLabs Voice Library, Notion Templates) has converged on community library models. Content Studio has the foundation (isBuiltIn pattern on style presets, loadPersonaByIdSafe cross-user loader) but no sharing/discovery layer. Proposed phased approach: Phase 1 built-in starter personas, Phase 2 publish+browse+clone, Phase 3 extend to style presets.
evidence: packages/db/src/schemas/personas.ts (no visibility/sharing columns), packages/db/src/schemas/style-presets.ts (isBuiltIn exists but no user sharing), packages/media/src/persona/use-cases/ (all ownership-scoped), docs/architecture/access-control.md (collaborator pattern exists but not applied to personas)
followUp: Issue #233 created: https://github.com/brandonbryant12/content-studio/issues/233. Awaiting human ready-for-dev label. Next path hypothesis: meso scope on persona/style-preset domain, evaluate built-in starter persona seeding as Phase 1 bounded slice.
reflection: External research across 5 platforms provided strong cross-platform convergence on three-tier visibility and deep-clone patterns. The existing isBuiltIn pattern on style presets is directly reusable infrastructure.
feedback: Future product-vision runs should check for this issue and debate amendments rather than creating duplicates.
